resources.string: No computer? No Internet? No problem. Code apps anywhere.
If you\'re facing issues while using Code on the Go or with building Android apps, you can join the Telegram Group and ask for help there.\n\nYou can also send suggestions on our email.
Download Code on the Go from the official website to get the latest features and updates.
No browser found to handle the request.
%1$s requires at least %2$dGB of free space to run and extract the necessary tools.\n\nPlease free up space on your device and try again.
Local model is not loaded. Open AI Settings, pick a model, and try again.
The AI backend is not ready. Please review your AI settings.
Error: Failed to load local model.
The planner could not produce a valid tool invocation after multiple attempts. Provide a direct textual answer without calling any tools.
The previous plan was rejected because the model attempted to call a tool even though no tool calls were allowed at that time.
The previous plan failed because the tool invocation was malformed.
The previous plan was rejected due to an invalid tool invocation.
Tool `%1$s` is now blocked until you can explain how to call it safely.

%1$s%2$s Before attempting another tool call, double-check the required parameters and confirm that calling a tool is necessary. If the request can be answered conceptually, respond with text instead of forcing a tool call.
Planner output was invalid. Asking it to correct the plan...
A function call without a name was emitted. Provide a valid tool name.
Unknown tool `%1$s` was requested. Use only the supported tools.
Tool `%1$s` is temporarily blocked after a malformed invocation. Choose a different tool or respond with a textual answer.
The previous plan violated tool requirements:\n
Re-plan with valid tool arguments, or provide a textual response if tools are unnecessary.
Planner proposed invalid tool usage. Requesting a corrected plan...
Please fill in all required fields (marked with *).
Entry \'%1$s\' not found in assets zip file
Missing installation files. %1$s installation might be corrupt or incomplete.
The picked file is not a directory.
Not enough storage available for installation. An additional %1$.1fGB is required on the internal storage partition. You currently have %2$.1fGB available.
Storage is not accessible. Please ensure the device is unlocked and try again.
Terminal setup failed. Storage space is insufficient, or files were removed during installation. Please free up some space and try again.
Terminal installation is only supported for the primary user.
You are using %1$s variant of Code On The Go on a %2$s-only device. This configuration is not supported. Please install the %2$s variant of Code On The Go.
Gradle files have changed. Sync the project to update dependencies and settings.
Initializing a project may take a few moments.
Gradle wrapper is not available.\nInstalling a default one. Gradle v7.4 will be downloaded if necessary.
Code on the Go is initializing your project. Please be patient!\n\nWhen setup is finished, \"Project initialized\" will be displayed at the bottom of the screen.
Do you want to open the last opened project? The project was:\n%s
Failed to start dragging the file
When you close the project, all build tasks will be stopped and unsaved changes to your project file may be lost. Do you want to save the files before you close the project?
If the file path includes a folder separator (\'/\'), any missing parent folders will be created automatically.
Are you sure you want to delete: \n%s?
Enter a new name for the file/folder.
Clear Gradle caches directory. This will delete all dependencies downloaded by Gradle. Required files will be downloaded again in next build.
Choose additional Gradle flags to execute with every task.
Set the default font size (measured in sp).
Choose which non-printing characters (like tabs, spaces, and line breaks) are displayed.
Break long lines of text or code into multiple lines so they fit in the visible window.
Long-press and hold to zoom in on the area around the cursor.
When enabled, restarting Code on the Go will automatically open the last project that was open.
When enabled, Code on the Go asks for confirmation before opening the last project.
If checked, \'/system/bin/sh\' will be used in terminal.
Set the number of spaces that the tab character indents.
Please choose default font size of editor :

This will delete caches directory created by Gradle. If you delete it, the next build may take a bit longer than normal as Gradle will download required files again.\n\nAre you sure you want to delete caches?
No views have been added. Click to add a view.
Selected file is not a valid Android XML layout.
We could not read this layout XML. %1$s
We could not open this layout XML. %1$s
We could not open this layout. %1$s
%1$s can have only one direct child. Wrap your views in a container like LinearLayout or ConstraintLayout.
We could not place %1$s inside %2$s. Check the view hierarchy.
We could not open this layout. Fix the XML and try again.
Swipe up to display logs, diagnostics, debug info, and more
Could not create a root view. The XML is empty or missing a valid top-level element.

An internal error has occurred.

Details to help us fix the problem have been saved and will be sent securely the next time your device is online.

When you press OK, the app will close and any unsaved changes may be lost. Please restart the app to continue.

If the error happens again, try restarting your device. If the problem persists, please %1$s

Build Number - %1$s

Please describe what you were doing when the app crashed:
Click on the button below to close this page.
Gemini API key is missing. Add it in AI Settings.
Local model is not loaded. Load a model in AI Settings.
Some files are not saved. Would you like to save them before closing? Unsaved files are: \n%s
When enabled, code completion matches class names and members even when typed in lowercase (case-insensitive matching).
Use Google Java Style code formatting
Format Java code using Google’s conventions.
Disable text predictions and auto-correction for the on-screen keyboard.
Specify a custom Gradle installation to use for build. This OVERRIDES the version specified in gradle-wrapper.properties.
Leave empty to use Gradle wrapper.
Build failed. See the Build Output panel for more details.
Failed to get text from editor
Select a directory from primary storage
When enabled, the editor considers blank space to be the boundary between words.
To view the file tree and project options, swipe from left to right or tap the menu icon at the upper left.
To view logs, diagnostics, and more, swipe up or tap the bottom of the page.
To view help for the Code on the Go app, long-press anything to view a tooltip. In the editor, select and long-press text to show the editing toolbar and help button.
To view help in Code on the Go, long-press anything to view a tooltip. In the editor, select and long-press text to show the editing toolbar and help button.
Choose whether the tab key inserts spaces instead of the tab character.
Discuss features, bugs, and improvements here.
More about Code on the Go
Remove empty new line characters at the end of a file.
Insert a single new line character at the end of a file.
Place each attribute on a new line.
Combine multiple CDATA blocks into a single block.
Combine multiple single-line XML comments into a single line.
Combine multiple lines of text in a single XML element into a single line.
Add a single space before every closing tag.
Preserve tags that appear on otherwise-empty lines instead of collapsing them.
Keep attributes on separate lines instead of collapsing them.
Place closing brackets and self-closing tags on a new line after the last attribute.
Remove extra whitespace at the end of lines.
Set the maximum number of characters on a single line before the editor automatically wraps or breaks the line.
Set the number of spaces to automatically indent attributes that are split on separate lines.
Set the maximum number of blank new lines that are kept when the file is reformatted or saved.
Choose how empty XML elements are formatted.
Set formatting preferences for XML files.
If enabled, the file at \"/data/data/com.itsaky.androidide/files/home/.cg/ui/font.ttf\" will be used as Editor font.
Your app is installed. Do you want to launch the app?
Code on the Go Gradle build service
The character \'_\' cannot be the first character in a package segment
A digit cannot be the first character in a package segment
The character "%1$s" is not allowed in Android application package names.
The package must have at least one \'.\' separator.
Unable to generate setters and getters
Unable to find any overridable method
A constructor with the same parameter types is already available
The following tasks will be run in order. Click the \'Run\' button again to confirm.\n\n%1$s
Select an attribute from the below list
Choose color scheme to use in editor
Choose whether to use light mode, dark mode, or the current system settings.
Select a theme for Code on the Go (requires restart).
You must build and install the app before you can launch it.
(EXPERIMENTAL) Whether the Java source files should be analyzed for errors or not.
Tooling API server is unavailable. Check build output and IDE logs for errors.
If a line contains no visible text characters, pressing backspace removes the entire line.
Remove a full indent level instead of just one character when you press backspace.
Checkout conflict: Please commit or stash your local changes before pulling. Conflicting files:\n%1$s
Allows this app to bind to Code on the Go\'s logging service. This permission is needed for Code on the Go to read this app\'s logs.
Must be path to a file
Must be path to a directory
Use Kotlin Script (.kts) for Gradle build files

Code on the Go uses Firebase Analytics and Sentry to help us improve the app.

Firebase Analytics collects anonymous usage data to help us understand how the app is used. 

Sentry helps us track and fix errors.

No personal information is collected or shared. All data is processed in accordance with our privacy policy.
Experimental/debugging options for Code on the Go
Dump Code on the Go logs to $HOME/.cg/logs
Run the debug variant of your application to view its logs here.
LogSender is disabled. You can enable it in preferences.
Logs from the IDE are shown here.
Open a file to show its diagnostic results
"Build the application or run a task to see its build output here. "
When disabled, logs from applications won\'t be shown in Code on the Go
Keep the current scope header pinned at the top of the editor while you scroll.
If enabled, the IDE will (without confirmation) launch the application right after it is installed.
Cannot run application. Unable to determine package name.
Cannot run application. No application modules found in project.

You are using %1$s variant of Code On The Go on a %2$s device. This configuration may work, but it\'ll result in reduced performance and possible security vulnerabilities. Please avoid using this configuration whenever possible.
Locks line numbers in view so they stay visible when scrolling horizontally.
Choose the language for Code on the Go.
Selected file is not a directory
Could not save the font file. Please try again.
A file save operation is already in progress!
\nTo install the tools needed to build Android projects, tap the button at the bottom right of the screen\n
No computer? No Internet? No problem.
Allow Code on the Go to open and save files on this device.
Allow Code on the Go to install the apps that you build on this device.
Allow Code on the Go to display floating debugger controls to inspect your code while it runs.
Floating debugger is not available on this device.
If you can\'t enable this permission, display App info for Code on the Go, tap the three-dot menu, and enable restricted settings.
Allow Code on the Go to display notifications.
Allow Code on the Go to access accessibility services for debugging.
• Notifications (to receive status messages) On Android 12 and earlier, notifications are enabled already.
• Storage (to create project directories and files)
• Install packages (to install the apps that you build)

• Overlay window (to use the debugger) If you can\'t enable this permission, go to Settings \u2192 Apps \u2192 Code on the Go. In the upper right Options menu, tap Enable restricted settings. Return to the installer and assign the permission. (The steps may vary on different devices.)
Code on the Go requires the following permissions:
Please grant the permissions to continue.
It seems you\'re offline. Check your internet connection.
Heads up! You\'re using cellular data.
Heads up! You\'re on a metered network.
Disable background data restriction to avoid installation failures.
Cannot create session. New sessions are disabled.
It\'s not just an IDE, it\'s a community. Contribute code, ideas, and passion to Code On The Go.
No application available to handle intent.
Code on the Go setup complete.
Please complete the IDE setup to continue.
Failed to start debugger. See IDE logs for more details.
The app is not paired. Tap the debugger button to start wireless ADB pairing.
The app is successfully paired. Tap the debugger button to build and install a debuggable version.
The value of variable \'%1$s\' cannot be updated.
Unable to listen for JDWP connections. Operation failed with error %1$d.
Code On the Go is not allowed to open or bind a network socket on the local network.
Network access for Code On the Go is blocked by the system. The app is not allowed to use the network or the service is unavailable.
Unable to start Code On the Go debugger.
The debugger is not ready for a debug session.

Please ensure Code On the Go has permissions to access the local network. This can be checked by going to the "App info" screen of Code On the Go in your device\'s settings, and checking if network access is allowed under \"Data usage\" (exact name might differ between devices).
If network access is allowed and the issue still persists, please file a bug report with the logs attached.
To send a private message to feedback@appdevforall.org, tap OK. If you aren’t connected to the Internet, the message will not be sent immediately. Do you want to continue?

Please enter your feedback in the space below. If you are reporting a problem, please include as much information as possible about the issue.

Thanks!

-------------stack trace----------
%1$s
Stack trace unavailable. Please describe the issue you encountered.
Click the New project button to create a new project, or open an existing project from a folder.
You don\'t have any recent projects
There are no projects available to delete.
No valid Android projects found in %1$s
Some folders were skipped because they are not valid Android project folders.
Cannot access folder %1$s. Check storage permissions.
The folder %1$s does not contain any subfolders.
Click the New project button to create a new project.
%s is not a valid Android project folder. Please open another folder instead.
Are you sure want to delete the selected project(s)? This action cannot be undone.
To report issues, make suggestions, and ask questions, email us at feedback@appdevforall.org. Internet access is required.
Feedback for Code on the Go
The log is too large to send directly.
There are files to commit, commit canceled
No files to commit, commit canceled
Committed all changes to repository in %1$s

<!DOCTYPE html>
        <html>
        <head>
            <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
                <style>
                     body {
                        margin: 0;
                        padding: 10px;
                        word-wrap: break-word;
                        color: %1$s;
                     }
                     a{
                        color: %3$s;
                        text-decoration: underline;
                       }
                </style>
            </head>
            <body>
                %2$s
            </body>

		</html>
This documentation is provided by a third-party plugin.
A build is already in progress. Ignoring new request.
No output listing file found in project model.
No APK found in output listing file.
APK file specified does not exist: %1$s
Build was cancelled by the user.
Plugin \'%1$s\' built successfully. Would you like to install it now?
Plugin installed. Restart the app to activate it.
Plugin changes will take effect after restarting the app. Do you want to restart now?
Could not delete the installation file
Unsupported file type. Please select a .cgp file
\n\nProject creation finished with warnings/errors. Open IDE Logs for details.
Tap the Back button to return to Code on the Go.
Can\'t connect to wireless debugging service.
Unable to generate key for wireless debugging.\nThe device may have a broken Keystore mechanism.
A notification from Code on the Go will help you complete the pairing. Please allow Code on the Go to show notifications with the \'%1$s\' notification channel.
Please enable wireless debugging and follow the instructions to pair the debugger.
Connecting to ADB daemon on port: %1$d…
Code on the Go is not allowed to start foreground services (OP_START_FOREGROUND denial)
The debugger requires Android 11 or higher to work.
Read the full debugger setup guide. To use the debugger, you must enable developer options. When you’re ready to pair with the debugger:

string-array.name: debugger_setup_pairing_steps
string-array.item: Tap Start pairing. Developer options are displayed.
Tap the words Wireless debugging to display more options.
When prompted, enter the pairing code. A success message will be displayed. When you return to Code on the Go, the debugger will be connected.

resources.string: Some devices (including older Xiaomi devices) also require giving Code on the Go the “Unrestricted battery usage permission”. See more information including troubleshooting tips.

This AI agent is experimental and may give incorrect or harmful answers. Always back up your work before you try it, and always double-check its suggestions before using them. We are not responsible for any errors, bugs, or damage caused by its output. Use at your own risk.
❌ No Gemini API Key found.\nIf you recently updated or reinstalled the app, your API key may have been reset.\nPlease add your Gemini key again in AI Settings.
Failed to save bitmap to file
Failed to capture or save screenshot
Back to Code on the Go

You are about to leave Code on the Go to open an external link:

%s

Do you want to continue?
⚠️ Experimental AI. Use at your own risk.
Add @Preview annotation to a @Composable function to see it here
Build the project to enable multi-file preview support
This commit has not been pushed to the remote repository
This commit has been pushed to the remote repository
No internet connection. Please check your network and try again.
Connection lost during download. Please check your internet connection and try again.
Set the name and email used for commits
Used as author name in commits
Used as author email in commits
Configure Git author in Preferences to enable commits.\nClick on the avatar for more info.
You can update your Git configuration in Preferences.
This project is not a Git repository
Push rejected: Your branch is behind the remote. Please pull to resolve conflicts before pushing.
Enter your credentials to sync changes with the remote repository.
Authentication failed. Ensure your Personal Access Token has \'repo\' permissions, or try clearing and re-entering credentials.
Pull completed with conflicts!\nPlease select the conflicted files from the list, resolve the highlighted issues in the editor, save file(s) and then commit your changes.
Are you sure you want to abort the current merge? All conflict resolutions will be discarded.
Skipping suspicious template entry outside project dir: %1$s
Pebble parse error in %1$s at line %2$d: %3$s
Pebble evaluation error in %1$s at line %2$d: %3$s
Failed to load template at %1$s error: %2$s
Failed to load template archive %1$s error: %2$s
